title: Dianthus andronakii
taxonomy: Dianthus andronakii is classified as a species within the genus Dianthus, which belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. This family is a part of the order Caryophyllales, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the carnation or pink family. The order Caryophyllales is a part of the clade core eudicots, which is a group of flowering plants thought to have evolved from a common ancestor around 125 million years ago. The clade core eudicots is a part of the larger clade angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its morphology and genetic makeup.
